issue_title: Esri cacher doesn't handle 'join' function correctly
issue_body:
The Esri cacher [tries to figure out which fields to request](https://github.com/openaddresses/machine/blob/master/openaddr/cache.py#L330) by inspecting the `conform` object of the source. When it encounters a `dict`, it [looks for the `field` attribute](https://github.com/openaddresses/machine/blob/master/openaddr/cache.py#L321) and adds that to the list of columns to request. In the case of the `join` function, which specifies the name should be `fields`, we end up with a `None` in the list of fields and this function fails.

The fix is to add `field` or `fields`.

issue_title: Datestamps for embedded resources appear to be use the base URI's datestamp
Add generator metadata to cdxj files produces by ipwb replay
ServiceWorker attempts to process HTML memento if installed upon access
issue_body:
Logic exists in the replay system (where?) to check if the ServiceWorker has been installed upon accessing a memento. This would occur if the replay system is started up for the first time and a URI-M is immediately accessed instead of the root page of the replay system.

ipwb current checks if it's installed and if not, redirects to install the SW then back to the originally accessed URI-M. Upon returning to the URI-M, the SW is processing the HTML page (root memento) and attempting to unsuccessfully reroute.

The console looks like this on first access using the above pattern:
![screen shot 2017-06-23 at 3 29 36 pm](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2514780/27497477-dbd1f502-5828-11e7-94f4-25b51ed31c3a.png)

and like this on subsequent accesses:
![screen shot 2017-06-23 at 3 30 24 pm](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2514780/27497490-ecc2b216-5828-11e7-9bcc-51b52875310f.png)

The errors _only occur_ if the SW is installed upon first access, not if the user navigates from a link on the main replay page.

The issue is likely in checking for `isNavigation = event.request.mode === 'navigate'`. Normally, going from the main replay page to this page would be a navigational event but with the redirection to install the SW then redirecting back, `event.request.mode` is `no-cors`.

This is a minor issue. Replay still works as expected (the attempt to recursively redirect the replay system URI at http://127.0.0.1:5000/memento/20170301192639/http://127.0.0.1:5000/20170301192639/cs.odu.edu/~mkelly/semester/2017_spring/remotefroggie.html fails) but this issue might also come up if a user first installs then accesses a URI, e.g., through following the README instructions.

issue_title: Automatic in-place override without caching for targeted module or all modules
issue_body:
Hi,

I was considering peru for my project. It seem pretty nice. However, I couldn't find any 
quick way to get a subset of modules described in the *.yaml file cloned directly to
their target location with their *.git (or *.hg) sub folder so that I can interract with
them.

I saw there is an override command but one has to clone his git / hg repository in
a separate step. This separate step seems superfluous most of the time as
the url mentioned in the *.yaml is exactly where I want to clone/pull/push from.

As a first step, it seem to me that it would greatly help if I could simply do
`peru override add --all` and have real clone put at their respective target locations using original url
for every module listed in the *.yaml file (hg, git, svn, etc; only the simple cases
where there are no complicated rules).

As a second step, it would be nice to be able to add modules to groups so
that the override command could target one or many groups (e.g.: `peru override add --group mygroup`).

It could also be nice to have a way to force using override from the *.yaml file systematically.

With these options, one can develop a package and its dependencies under the same
working directory and have all syncing deferred to peru (sync would stop on error).

Would there be any difficulties implementing theses enhancements?

issue_title: Importing pytest_* modules should not trigger PluginValidationError
issue_body:
In https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest-bdd/issues/224 @soulrebel had an issue when [importing `pytest_bdd` in his `conftest.py` file](https://github.com/tierratelematics/cookiecutter-qa/blob/916df0a10f8a934a655b33b8de01d788e851fbb0/%7B%7Bcookiecutter.project_slug%7D%7D/%7B%7Bcookiecutter.project_slug%7D%7D/tests/functional/conftest.py#L4):

```
_pytest.vendored_packages.pluggy.PluginValidationError: unknown hook 'pytest_bdd' in plugin <module 'project_qa.tests.functional.conftest' (<_pytest.assertion.rewrite.AssertionRewritingHook object at 0x7f035b2d4f28>)>
```

This seems odd - importing a `pytest_*` module should work normally, no? Maybe pluggy should at least check that unknown hooks aren't module objects?

I noticed that the tests [here](https://github.com/pytest-dev/pluggy/blob/master/testing/test_method_ordering.py#L183) and [here](https://github.com/pytest-dev/pluggy/blob/master/testing/test_hookrelay.py#L63) appear to be essentially duplicates under different names?
Is there any reason I should not remove one of them?

issue_title: @profile decorator does not work on two or more flask routes
issue_body:
When one tries to use @profile on two or more flask routes/functions this error is thrown:

`AssertionError: View function mapping is overwriting an existing endpoint function: wrapper
`
The solution is to use @wraps in the decorator definition.
Can we add @wraps in the profile decorator definition?
I posted a stack-overflow question and solution here:
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/48193461/use-memory-profiler-to-profile-lines-in-a-flask-app/48193527#48193527

It should be possible to run it on a single file / path only.

Additionally, `dodgy --help` should not run, but display help.

To skip best practice format related linting. 

Also make it a settable default in ``~/.planemo.yml``.

See also:
- https://github.com/galaxy-iuc/standards/issues/3#issuecomment-95586134

_From @jacquerie on April 21, 2017 10:22_

Currently the API of `dojson` is part in https://github.com/inspirehep/inspire-next/tree/271c1a0936dd188c97f9fe7ae1b365ab0368ff25/inspirehep/dojson/utils, part in https://github.com/inspirehep/inspire-next/blob/271c1a0936dd188c97f9fe7ae1b365ab0368ff25/inspirehep/dojson/processors.py.

It should ideally all live in `inspirehep/dojson/api.py`, and all `utils` that are used elsewhere should be moved in a higher scope (for example: `validate` should live in `inspire_schemas`).

_Copied from original issue: inspirehep/inspire-next#2248_

There might be a discrepancy on what type the `full_name` has when it leaves `dojson`. 

#### Why
Well, firstly from @iulianav 's PR and the discussion [here](https://github.com/inspirehep/inspire-next/pull/3064#discussion_r161750708) we got the first idea that this is happening (with her latest commit).  

Secondly, after a discussion with @jacquerie IRL, we had the impression that it was `inspire_utils.name::normalize_name` that had the issue.

But then in [this commit](https://github.com/inspirehep/inspire-utils/pull/39/commits/60f1ad1f9bee107e97bc7530ceebfd1a89c13a1c), after I converted all string literals in the `expected_name` from `str` to `unicode`, all the tests pass (without me having to change anythin in the `name` module, which means that `normalize_name` does return `unicode`.

One of the undocumented features of jpype is that it is possible to call a base class method using the class wrapper.  However, this results in a segfault in the event that the base class method is abstract.  The problem appears to be that the JDK messes up the call stack if CallNonvirtual* is executed on an abstract method.  Thus rather than issuing an error, it goes to abort.

```
import jpype
from jpype import JString
jpype.startJVM(jpype.getDefaultJVMPath(), '-Djava.class.path=test/classes')
ArrayList = jpype.JClass('java.util.ArrayList')
Iterator = jpype.JClass('java.util.Iterator')
arrayList = ArrayList()
arrayList.add(1)
arrayList.add(2)
iter = arrayList.iterator()
print(Iterator.next(iter))
```

issue_title: Letsencrypt cannot deal with apache's WSGI configuration
issue_body:
Hello, I found current letsencrypt-auto has an issue with WSGIDaemonProcess config.

This is part of my apache2 config in _/etc/apache2/sites-enabled/003-ctf.conf_, which connects to django using WSGI.

```
WSGIDaemonProcess kctf python-path=/var/www/ctf:/var/www/ctf/kctfenv/lib/python2.7/site-packages
WSGIProcessGroup kctf
WSGIScriptAlias / /var/www/ctf/kctf/wsgi.py
```

This config forms default http connection.
When letsencrypt is run, it copies .conf file to another .conf file, then add ssl configurations, then restarting apache2 instantly.
However, WSGIDaemonProcess cannot be duplicated in .conf files, so letsencrypt-auto faces reboot failure and fall back.

This is because WSGIDaemonProcess which has same group name must exist in 'one' file.
If it exists in http conf file, it must be not in https conf file.

Currently, before running letsencrypt-auto, I must comment WSGIDaemonProcess temporary.
After running letsencrypt-auto, I uncomment WSGIDaemonProcess in http conf file and restart once more.
This way, WSGIDaemonProcess is commented in https conf file, so apache2 do not suffer from restart failure.

I hope letsencrypt gets better by fixing this problem.

issue_title: Install/compile fails of letsencrypt-auto on Smartos/Illumos
issue_body:
The auto method fails because a script was not written.

Can't seem to find Augeas in the package manager. It may be possible to install letsencrypt client manually.

The spec has added support for a `meta` section in the `directory` object which allows for a number of pieces of information that are not necessarily related to the ACME protocol. Currently the client will fail if these fields are returned which is causing letsencrypt/boulder#1692.

Also in general it would be good to be slightly less strict about JSON deserialization (i.e. ignoring unexpected fields) since we have other better ways of testing if the server/client start drifting out of sync.

I'm hitting what appears to be a logic error in the `dehydrate` method of `ToManyField` class in _fields.py_:
The code in question starts at line 856: 
```
        if the_m2ms is None:
            if not self.null:
                raise ApiFieldError("The model '%r' has an empty attribute '%s' and doesn't allow a null value." % (previous_obj, attr))

        if isinstance(the_m2ms, models.Manager):
            the_m2ms = the_m2ms.all()

        m2m_dehydrated = [
            self.dehydrate_related(
                Bundle(obj=m2m, request=bundle.request),
                self.get_related_resource(m2m),
                for_list=for_list
            )
            for m2m in the_m2ms
        ]
```
Let's say `the_m2ms` is `None` and `self.null` is `False`. The code will not raise an error, will not execute the `if isinstance` clause and will land on the `for` iteration. At this point a `TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not iterable` is thrown.

To replicate this, create a `ModelResource` subclass  with a self-referencing related field:
```class MyResource(ModelResource):
    children = fields.ToManyField('self', 'children', null=True, full=True)```

issue_title: DefaultLogger::detatchStream return value seems not to match documentation
issue_body:
The docs for `DefaultLogger::detatchStream` say that it returns true if the user stream is detached and we gain ownership, but that seems not to be the case in my project.

This is how I attach the stream (g_logger is a `std::unique_ptr`):
```
auto severity = Logger::Err | Logger::Warn;
DefaultLogger::get()->attachStream(g_logger.get(), severity);
```

This is how I try to detach it:
```
bool result = DefaultLogger::get()->detatchStream(g_logger.get(), 0);
```
Passing 0 as severity level is correct, is it not?

Glancing through the implementation of `DefaultLogger::detatchStream`, it seems that the function does not return `true` because of the `break` statement before it.

The strangest thing is that my usage was working fine when I first wrote it, but it broke after I fixed a bug elsewhere in the project ...

issue_title: datastore query docs don't use datastore.Query shortcuts
issue_body:
See http://googlecloudplatform.github.io/gcloud-python/latest/datastore-queries.html#gcloud.datastore.query.Query.fetch

The snippet is

```python
>>> from gcloud.datastore.query import Query
>>> query = Query('dataset-id', 'Person')
>>> query.add_filter('name', '=', 'Sally')
>>> list(query.fetch())
[<Entity object>, <Entity object>, ...]
>>> list(query.fetch(1))
[<Entity object>]
```

I'd expect it to be

```python
>>> from gcloud import datastore
>>> query = datastore.Query('Person') # note no dataset id
>>> query.add_filter('name', '=', 'Sally')
>>> list(query.fetch())
[<Entity object>, <Entity object>, ...]
>>> list(query.fetch(1))
[<Entity object>]
```

and a separate example for a specific dataset

```python
>>> from gcloud import datastore
>>> dataset = datastore.Dataset('dataset-id')
>>> query = dataset.Query('Person')
>>> query.add_filter('name', '=', 'Sally')
>>> list(query.fetch())
[<Entity object>, <Entity object>, ...]
>>> list(query.fetch(1))
[<Entity object>]
```

Page Name: resource-manager-api
Release: ee835e9

We have

```python
>>> from gcloud import resource_manager

>>> # List all projects you have access to
>>> client = resource_manager.Client()
>>> for project in client.list_projects():
...     print(project)
```

and then client is reused a bunch. Might be better to have

```python
>>> # Create a client to talk to the Resource Manager API
>>> from gcloud import resource_manager
>>> client = resource_manager.Client()

>>> # List all projects you have access to
>>> for project in client.list_projects():
...     print(project)
```
Like dataset.get, there should be a dataset.delete and dataset.put that accepts a list of keys / entities.

issue_title: The Python BigQuery client fails on creating a view in standardSQL
issue_body:
```python
from google.cloud import bigquery

bigquery_client = bigquery.Client(project="my-project")
dataset = bigquery_client.dataset("my-dataset")

table = dataset.table("v_test")

#Fails on Standard SQL
#table._properties['view'] = {'query': "select * from `my-project.my.dataset.test_table`",'useLegacySql':False}  

#Works on Legacy SQL
table._properties['view'] = {'query': "select * from [my-project:my-dataset.test_table]",'useLegacySql':True}  

try:
    table.create()
except Exception as err:
    print(err)
```
The https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/bigquery/google/cloud/bigquery/table.py#L355 should also take in another property for useLegacySql

Example  payload at http://stackoverflow.com/questions/33243427/how-can-i-create-a-new-view-in-bigquery-using-the-python-api

@tseaver suggested I start a new issue after adding to a closed issue: https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/issues/3191

BigQuery Job Objects are not pickle-able:

```

In [16]: job = client.copy_table(source_table,source_table)

In [17]: job
Out[17]: <google.cloud.bigquery.job.CopyJob at 0x1163a3320>

In [18]: pickle.dumps(job)
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
AttributeError                            Traceback (most recent call last)
<ipython-input-18-0c1255e2d696> in <module>()
----> 1 pickle.dumps(job)

AttributeError: Can't pickle local object 'if_exception_type.<locals>.if_exception_type_predicate'
```

I'm guessing that's because it represents some sort of future? IMO it would be fine if `__getstate__` deleted the polling and let a reference that could be checked.

@tseaver suggests: 

> As a workaround, you could pickle the result of job._to_api_repr, and then reconstitute it using client.job_from_resource.

which works. This could also be done by `__getstate__` itself.

Thanks

issue_title: Client library incorrectly returns error when FLAC is used with long-running-recognize
issue_body:
The client library incorrectly returns an error when anything other than LINEAR16 is used for a long-running recognize request:

https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/speech/google/cloud/speech/sample.py#L173

The v1 server support all encodings so this breaks functionality.

In general, the client library should almost never do it's own validation; it should always delegate to the server to be sure that that behavior and error messages are consistent across APIs.

> Python version 3.6.1 |Anaconda 4.4.0 (64-bit)| (default, May 11 2017, 13:25:24) [MSC v.1900 64 bit (AMD64)]
> nbgrader version 0.5.3

### `jupyterhub --version` (if used with JupyterHub)
N/A

### Notebook - Help, About version info
> The version of the notebook server is 5.0.0

### Expected behavior
An exervise notebook reads an external file (e.g. data/data.txt) and autograding tests depend on reading this file. I edit the `source/` version before distributing.   All tests pass when running the notebook with Run All. I hit `Validate` and expect to see the same result.

### Actual behavior
If a notebook accesses an external file, validation will fail if Jupyter was launched from the root of the nbgrader directory (or anywhere except the specific directory where the notebook is). This is because Validate seems to set/(or be run from) the current directory to the launch dir of Jupyter.

This also happens with the `release` version (validation will fail even if the solution is correct, because the external files will not be accessible).

### Steps to reproduce the behavior
Create a new assignment.
Add any file (e.g. "data/data.txt"). It doesn't have to be in a subdirectory for this to happen.
Add an autograder answer and test like:

      ### BEGIN SOLUTION
      with open("data/data.txt") as f:
             contents = f.read()
      ### END SOLUTION

      # test
      # will fail because contents will not be defined
      assert(len(contents)>0)

Alternatively, just create an autograder test:
   
     import os
     raise(Exception(os.getcwd()))

Hit `Validate`
The current working directory will be the launch directory of Jupyter, and not the notebook directory. 

I can't see an obvious workaround for this. Apologies if I am missing something obvious.

issue_title: Shortcut Key in File Editor to Block Comment
issue_body: Many text editors (see for example [Sublime](https://stackoverflow.com/a/11443903/2638485)) have a shortcut key that block comments the selected lines of code. Given the file editor has as language selection, this language can be used to properly block comment. ie in Python this would just be prepending each line in the selection with a `#`.

I have successfully managed to do the exact same date & log group export using aws cli.  

Using the same values (dates in milliseconds, folder paths etc) I have been wracking my brains trying to figure out the reason why aws cli example of create-export-task and boto3 create_export_task appear to both successfully execute and complete but only the aws cli actually places logs in the S3 Bucket.

boto3 in /usr/local/lib/python3.5/dist-packages (1.7.45)
DISTRIB_ID=LinuxMint
DISTRIB_RELEASE=18.3
Python 3.5.2

boto3 code extract:

            cwSession = boto3.Session(profile_name=self.params['profileName'], region_name=self.cw['region'])
            cwClient  = cwSession.client('logs')

            cwResponse = cwClient.create_export_task(taskName=self.cw['taskName'],
                logGroupName=self.params['logGroupName'],
                logStreamNamePrefix=self.cw['lgnPrefix'],
                fromTime=self.cw['startDateEpoch'],
                to=self.cw['endDateEpoch'],
                destination=self.cw['dest'],

I've ensured that the startDateEpoch and endDateEpoch are in milliseconds and I'm pretty confident the boto3 is using the right profile.  I check the cwResponse and moves from RUNNING to COMPLETED and no exceptions. 

In the boto3 export_create_task version, what I see in the Cloudwatch logs panel is the tasks ran successfully and Cloudwatch will link to a non-existent (empty) S3 bucket where it thought the logs should be.  This directory then is replaced by "no objects" message and if you navigate up one folder in S3 the log directory is no longer present.  In the S3 GUI I see no such folder, only the permission test file "aws-logs-write-test".  There is no exported log directory:

issue_title: Do not hardcode directory for globus tokens
issue_body: Currently the token file is hardcoded to `TOKEN_FILE = 'runinfo/.globus.json'`. This will break if the user uses a non-default rundir. I vote not to couple it to the rundir so that it can be re-used between different scripts with different rundirs without requiring re-authentication, for example, `$HOME/.parsl/globus.json`.

issue_title: Context manager is "Acquisition" or "Generation" for all APIs
issue_body:
In each driver's metadata/config.py we specify "direction" (input or output).
The code generator keys off of that in order to decide what to call the Context Manager that we return on initiate().

```
def test_acquisition(session):
    session.configure_measurement_digits(nidmm.Function.DC_CURRENT, 1, 5.5)
    with session.initiate():
        session.fetch(1000)
```

It's not a big deal because the user rarely sees the class name. But for bidirectional APIs like NI-HSDIO (which won't be added anytime soon) and for NI-SWITCH, these names are meaningless. I propose the name of the context manager itself is specified in metadata rather than we try to deduce it based on direction.

Also to consider: If the classes are to be considered private, then perhaps they should be prefixed with _.

issue_title: Allow Open Browser keyword to pass capabilities also for local browsers
issue_body: Sometimes it could be useful to allow capabilities be passed also to local browsers. The current implementation of the `Open Browser` only passes the capabilities  to the Selenium side only when `remote_url` argument is used.

issue_title: Regressions in `Dismiss Alert` and `Confirm Action` compared to 1.8
issue_body:
At the moment (SL 3.0 beta 3) there are some regressions compared to version 1.8:

1. `Dismiss Alert    accept=${FALSE}` leaves the alert open but it in 1.8 it closes it regardless the value of `accept`. It's behavior to accept by default and require configuration to dismiss is strange, but we already have #933 about that and other alert related oddities.

2. `Dismiss Alert` always returns the message of the alert but in 1.8 it returns true or false depending was the alert close or not. This is strange behavior too.

3. `Confirm Action` accepts the first alert but subsequent alerts are dismissed by default. In 1.8 all alerts were accepted by default.

issue_title: Failing assertion (probably related to the use of QF_BV)
issue_body:
In the attached log, the debug version of Z3 fails with an assertion violation in the SAT solver's clause handling. The release version of Z3 works (i.e. hides the error without a seg fault), but valgrind reports an out-of-memory error and aborts the execution.

This issue arises only if the Z3 solver is configured for QF_BV. If we feed it the same problem and configure it for QF_UFBV, the problem does not occur.

[z3_assertion.zip](https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/files/225884/z3_assertion.zip)

The following benchmark uses an admittedly convoluted way of converting the single-precision float number `0.5` to a signed 64-bit value, using round-nearest-ties-to-even-mode:

```
(set-option :produce-models true)
(set-logic QF_FPBV)
(define-fun s2 () (_ FloatingPoint  8 24) ((_ to_fp 8 24) roundNearestTiesToEven (/ 1 2)))
(declare-fun s0 () (_ FloatingPoint  8 24))
(declare-fun s1 () (_ BitVec 64))
(assert
   (let ((s3 (fp.eq s0 s2)))
   (let ((s9 ((_ fp.to_sbv 64) roundNearestTiesToEven s0)))
   (let ((s12 (= (bvcomp s1 s9) #b1)))
   (let ((s13 (and s3 s12)))
   s13)))))
(check-sat)
(get-value (s1))
```

Z3 says:

```
sat
((s1 #x0000000000000001))
```

i.e., z3 converts the value to `1`. I believe the correct result should be `0` in this rounding-mode.

Folks,
i know 4.8 is work-in-progress, but i think any hint to open issues is welcome.
Attached are a small problem-file (z38x8-v30.txt) and the results of running it with 4.7 and the last two nightly builds of 4.8.
Statistics are at end of file.

[res-8x8-v30-v480b.txt](https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/files/2253689/res-8x8-v30-v480b.txt)
[res-8x8-v30-v471.txt](https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/files/2253690/res-8x8-v30-v471.txt)
[res-8x8-v30-v480a.txt](https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/files/2253691/res-8x8-v30-v480a.txt)
[z38x8-v30.txt](https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/files/2253692/z38x8-v30.txt)

issue_title: Solution to MaxSAT problem from Java API violates some assertions containing xor
Any way to retrieve mapping from bit-blasted CNF formula back to BitVector variables?
issue_body: I have used z3 in several cases, and find it very useful.  However, I am currently dealing with a problem where I need a fairly random distribution of satisfying assignments for a given BitVector problem.  This is exactly what the tool weightgen (https://bitbucket.org/kuldeepmeel/weightgen) does with CNF formulas.  Is there any way to extract a bit-blasted CNF formula from Z3, solve it using another solver (in this case weightgen), and plug the satisfying assignment back in to retrieve the solutions to the original problem?

Hi,

to reproduce the bug please run the following Python script:

```python
from z3 import *
f = Fixedpoint()
a = Bool('a:3')
f.register_relation(a.decl())
f.fact(a)
print f.to_string([a])
```
that produces the incorrect smt-lib string:
```
(declare-fun |a:3| () Bool)
(declare-rel a:3 ())
(rule |a:3|)
(query a:3)
```

There are 2 issues:

1. `a:3` is not quoted in both `declare-rel` and `query`
2. `declare-fun` should not be there

issue_title: crash with many axioms over reals
issue_body:
Hello Z3 team,

please look at this [file](https://gist.github.com/kanigsson/e95317d7ea5780bbd983). With the option "memory_max_alloc_count=400000", I get

    *** Error in `z3': double free or corruption (fasttop): 0x00000000028fb730 ***
    Aborted (core dumped)"

This is on x86_64-linux. I am sory for the size of the file, but removing any axiom seems to make the error disappear. I am only guessing, but the large number of axioms over reals might play a role.

Note that we are working on targeting the z3 support for floats directly at some point. This will make many of the axioms disappear I suppose.

The following benchmark was quickly answered `sat` up until a few days ago, but now z3 goes into an infinite loop on it. The breakage must've happened in the last few days:

```
(set-option :produce-models true)
(declare-fun x () Int)
(assert (>= 1 x))
(assert (<= 0 x))
(minimize x)
(maximize (+ x x))
(set-option :opt.priority pareto)
(check-sat)
(get-model)
```

I should like to suggest a new Z3 tactic that partitions a given SMT-problem (CSP) into minimal independent components. 

The partition algorithm works on the primal constraint graph. 
(1) If two variables appear together in a constraint, they end up in the same CSP-component. 
(2) A constraint that has a variable in one of the CSP-components, ends up in that component. 

The partition tactic should be a splitting tactic like split-clause. The constraints in each component should be combined into a separate goal. 

A tactical such as then could be used to serially solve each of the component-CSPs. 

A tactic such as par-then could be used to solve the component-CSPs in parallel. 
It would be very useful if one could limit the number of threads used by par-then.

On top of #49, we should differentiate between basic and advanced examples. The idea would be that the basic ones could serve as tutorials for how to set up something: a constraint, use re-optimization, etc.
The advanced ones would be more in the spirit of what we currently have as examples, that is, small applications showing how to solve some problem.

issue_title: Move semantic issue in operator() for pybind::array and pybind::array_t
issue_body:
Variadic template methods of `pybind::array` and `pybind::array_t` used for accessing data, namely

`data`, `mutable_data`, `offset_at`, `index_at`, `get_byte_offset `, `at`, `mutable_at `

should take arguments by value instead of universal reference.

The semantic in the standard library for functions taking integral or iterator arguments is to take them by value. (For example `std::vector<T>::operator[]` takes its argument by value).

issue_title: Passing /dev/fd/0 to az storage blob upload no longer works
issue_body:
### Description
With the azure CLI 2.0.12 and below, it was possible to upload a stream from STDIN to blob storage by an invocation like the following -

```
az storage blob upload -f /dev/fd/0 --max-connections 1 ....
```

Obviously this only worked on platforms that have `/dev/fd`, but otherwise this worked nicely to upload unknown-length streams we didn't want to buffer to disk.

This appears to have been broken since https://github.com/Azure/azure-cli/commit/7d51854504343b298f27d401e7bbf3bef8ffa039 - I suspect it's something to do with the MIME type detection. ### Description
Provide commands in Interactive mode to record activity and generate automation scripts. 

### Scenario 1 - "I want to automate something common"

```
$ az interactive

$ az script start

$ az command 1
[...]

$ grep command 2
[...]

$ az command 3
[...]

$ az command 4
[...]

$ az script stop
az command 1
grep command 2
az command 3
az command 4

$ az script export -n MyScript.sh
```

### Scenario 2 - "I do this everyday, please automate it"
Customer does some tasks daily, but would like to run them automatically.  The `--service-principal` will create a new service principal if one does not exist for the current subscription matching the sp name.  

```
# assumes start/stop from Scenario #1

$ az script export --service-principal myautomation
[Creating service principal: http://myautomation]
[Assigning contributor rights to subscription]
[Creating script]
az login --service-principal -u [] -p [] --tenant []
az command 1
grep command 2
az command 3
az command 4
az logout

$ az script export --service-principal myautomation -n MyScript.sh
```

### Scenario 3 - "I want to make shareable scripts"
I want to make scripts that are easy to share with the ecosystem or colleagues.


### Scenario 4 - "This isn't working, can you look at this script and help me?"

### Scenario 5 - "I want scripts based on good patterns"

```
# Generates a good starting template for the current platform
$ az script export --template
$ az script export --template --shell bash

$ az script export --error-handling 
$ az script export --check-exists 
```

## Command breakdown

### Script record
```
Command
    az script start: Starts capturing interactive-mode commands for generating scripts.

Arguments
    --append      : Continue a previous recording; by default, previous recordings are deleted
    --import        : Read from file (or std-in if not provided) and place contents in script
```

### Script stop

```
Command
    az script stop: Stops any active script recordings and outputs the current script history, unmodified.  

Arguments
    --clear      : Delete any existing recordings
```

### Script export

```
Command
    az script export: Exports the existing recorded script as a file

Arguments
    --type -t            : [Required] What format script should be generated.  
                                 Allowed values: bash, sh, bat, notebook
    --name  -n         : Filename to output script
    --template         : Outputs the default template
    --error-handing : Wrap CLI calls with error-handling logic
    --exist-check    : Check if resources already exists before creation.  If found, resource will be 
                                 updated, otherwise it will be created.
    --create-sp        : Name of a service-principal to use as an identity to run the script.  If the
                                 service-principal does not exist, one will be created with contributor rights
                                 to the current subscription.
    --remove-pii       : Attempts to remove private information from generated scripts, such as
                                 subscription IDs and command-line prompted passwords
    --use-params     : Extracts common CLI parameters and any referenced shell/env variables into 
                                  script parameters and includes a 'usage' string
```

issue_title: [WebApp] Delete command should be more transparent about deleting plans
issue_body:
### Description

If you call the `az webapp delete` command, and the deleted webapp instance is the last one within the App Service plan, then it will delete the plan as well. Personally, I found this behavior pretty confusing (since you have no way of knowing it's happening), but more importantly, it wasn't what I wanted at all, so I had to re-create the plan again.

The portal makes this behavior pretty clear when you're attempting to delete a web app instance, and it also allows you to prevent deleting the plan. It would be great if the CLI allowed suppressing the plan deletion behavior (some new flag?) and/or maybe it would make sense to display a prompt that notifies the user that they're about to delete the only web app, and ask whether they'd like to also delete the plan. That way, the behavior is clear and predictable.

---

issue_title: [DNS] 'az network dns record-set cname set-record' should be '... update' instead
issue_body:
The DNS 'SOA' and 'CNAME' record types are similar, in that they don't support multiple records in the same record set. For that reason, they can't use the same method as the other record types to update the existing record (for the existing types, we use 'add-record' to add a new record followed by 'remove-record' to remove the old one...that doesn't work since the record set must support 2 records at the same time.)

For 'SOA' we have an 'update' command. For 'CNAME' we have a 'set-record' command. But basically they do the same thing--update the existing record.

For consistency, the 'set-record' command for CNAME should be renamed as 'update'.

issue_title: az acs list should list all acs instances with resource group as an optional value
issue_body:
In most cases customers will place ACS instances in its own resource group as each acs instance creates a lot of resources. 
Yes, they can put multiples in one rg, However, having to add yet another parameter to get the norm feels overly complex. I can't speak for VM, vmss but I'd suggest we go the opposite standard. If I call az VM list, the default behavior is to list them all, and if I want a specific rg I pass it in as rg is a standard parameter. 

From @rgardler 
+1

I do not want acs commands to have to conform to a model designed for VMs. ACS is about managing containers, not VMS. Our users should not need to worry about the underlying Azure concepts such as resource group, VMSS, VM, NSG, LB etc. etc. Our job is to abstract away from these as much as possible.

Today ACS is only an IaaS service. We will always see customers using it as IaaS and enjoying the flexibility this brings, but at the same time we will be moving more and more towards a managed service and we should, wherever possible, define the CLI tools to account for this.

issue_title: QueueService class in azure-storage 0.34.3 not pickleable, so unusuable in a Python MP context
issue_body:
Hi,
I originally posted this in the azure-sdk-for-python repository, and was told this was the proper place.

Instances of the QueueService class in the azure-storage 0.34.3 repository appear to not be serializable by the pickle module in Python 2.7, and therefore it can't be used in a Python MP context (eg. in Process objects). The default decoding/encoding callback functions appear to be implemented in a static class QueueMessageFormat using @staticmethod methods, which appear not to be pickleable. 
A workaround is to implement custom "non-static" callbacks and set the instance fields in the QueueService object to these custom callbacks.

Perhaps this design pattern should be changed so the QueueService class is pickleable by default, or guidance could be given to implement your own encode/decode callbacks (forgive me if this is already documented).

Platform: Windows
Python version: 2.7.9 +

Reproducible source code scenario distilled from SDK code:

```python
import pickle

class QueueMessageFormat:
    @staticmethod
    def noencode(data):
        return data     
    @staticmethod
    def nodecode(data):
        return data
class QueueService(object):
    def __init__(self):
        self.encode_function = QueueMessageFormat.noencode
        self.decode_function = QueueMessageFormat.nodecode
def main():
    f=QueueService()
    with open("pickleme.txt","wb") as output:
        pickle.dump(f,output)
if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```
This example yields "pickle.PicklingError: Can't pickle <function noencode at 0x02880930>: it's not found as __main__.noencode"

Azure SDK install information ("pip freeze azure")

urllib3==1.21.1

*Problem*

Currently Client `__init__` method are:
```python
def __init__(
            self, credentials, subscription_id, api_version='2016-01-01', accept_language='en-US', long_running_operation_retry_timeout=30, generate_client_request_id=True, base_url=None, filepath=None):
```

We already found an issue with `long_running_operation_retry_timeout` being inconsistent. We want to remove all configuration parameters from the `__init__` to simplify the signature and be more open to new parameters in the future without re-generate the client. This reduce the file size too.

*Fix*

New signature will be:
```python
def __init__(self, credentials, subscription_id, api_version='2016-01-01'):
```

parameters are still available in the config attribute.
This code:
```python
client = Client(credentials, subscription_id, long_running_operation_retry_timeout=666)
```
becomes
```python
client = Client(credentials, subscription_id)
client.config.long_running_operation_retry_timeout = 666
```

Feature equivalent, but we pushed advanced configuration to be used as an attribute and not a `__init__` method. This allows future code parameters to also works out of the box with no regeneration, since the config attribute is manage by msrestazure directly.

Since this is a breaking change for users using advanced configuration settings, planned for next major release of msrestaure

FYI @derekbekoe @johanste @annatisch @brettcannon

issue_title: Give a better error when users make an async fixture non-function-scoped, or use it with a sync test
issue_body:
It would be nice if when Trio mode was activated, this gave an error:

```python
@pytest.fixture
async def my_async_fixture():
    ...

# Notice: not an async test
def test_something(my_async_fixture):
    ...
```

Obviously we can't do this when trio mode isn't activated, because someone else might be handling the fixture. But when it is, we could give a nice error in theory.

The blocker is that I'm not sure how we're supposed to check the pytest configuration from `pytest_fixture_setup`...

Also, it would also be nice if we could make it so when Trio mode is activated, this gives an error:

```python
@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
async def whatever():
    ...
```

This is tricky because if someone does do this, then from `pytest_setup_fixture`'s point of view, it actually *won't detect that this fixture should be treated as a trio fixture*, because for a session fixture we can't see which tests it will ultimately be used with (and detect that they have `@pytest.mark.trio`). So this actually ends up being similar to the above case.

(Unless there's some way to ask pytest for a list of all the items that a session-scoped fixture will ultimately be used for, but I don't know of one.)

One way:
Generate our reflection-y build_dictionary/goals_refeference docs with old-pants
and again with new-pants
diff the results

That might be noisy and hard to parse, though. So it might be nice if we had a way to generate lists of flags, targets, configs, ... without all of the doc verbiage and .rst formatting.

In OSS pants, 
`$ for i in `seq 10`; do time ./pants goals &> /dev/null; done` ranges from 1.2 to 1.5 seconds. 

In twitter, we consume pants in pex. sample data:
`$ for i in `seq 10`; do time ./pants goals &> /dev/null; done` ranges from 2.3 to 3 seconds. which is way too much for a simple operations like `./options` or `goals`.

The overhead not only comes from pants itself, but from unpacking pants from pex as well.

Hence I am proposing/ going to experiment the idea to spin a lightweight server that listens to incoming commands right before bootstrapping options (https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/1f554c8ca1fc56b4830091d7c75aac3056785e9e/src/python/pants/bin/pants_runner.py#L49-L49)

Comments / suggestions welcome!

issue_title: NuPIC could benefit from separate management of algorithm parameters.
issue_body:
Creating a Parameters object that can be applied to the initialization of the algorithms as one step in separating the algorithm state from the algorithms proper, should be considered. Benefits are:
1. This can be done whether or not state is pulled out of the algorithms.
2. One central place to see all the algorithm parameters.
3. Parameters can be applied to more than one setup with and the "applied" in cookie stamp fashion without having to manually configure each algorithm instantiation.
4. More intelligent use of defaults and more flexible application of parameters can be innovated (i.e. getting parameters from distributed locations).
5. Makes it easier to eventually develop "sanity protocols" for handling parameter expectations and methods that deal with this.
6. etc. etc. :-P ([perusal of HTM.Java's handling of this may be helpful](https://github.com/numenta/htm.java/blob/master/src/main/java/org/numenta/nupic/Parameters.java))

issue_title: folium.choropleth() does not use pandas series as key column correctly
issue_body:
**Set-up:** To give some idea of the set-up, I have a 2-column pandas DataFrame, called `german_cars_price`. The first column of this dataframe is labeled `state` and denotes the relevant German federal state. The second column is labeled `price` and refers to the average used car sales price in that German federal state from a sample of ads on eBay.

I also have a GeoJSON file, `data/federal_state_borders.geojson`, which gives the boundaries of the German federal states, and I have imported that into Python using `geopandas`, to get a (geo) DataFrame called `federal_state_borders`. The column in this DataFrame identifying the German federal states is called `VAR_NAME1`. It is a modified version of the geoJSON found [here](https://github.com/isellsoap/deutschlandGeoJSON) (my translations of the names into English are different).

For the full set-up of a minimal working example producing this problem, see here: https://github.com/krinsman/minimal_working_example
If nothing else, hopefully GitHub's display of the workbook can give someone a better idea of the structure of the relevant data, if that is necessary to understand the question.

The environment.yml and the Makefile to create the conda virtual environment from it is included for completeness, but it seems unlikely that this would be necessary in all cases.

**Problem:** Trying to create a choropleth map using this data leads to the error:    

    ValueError: The truth value of a Series is ambiguous. Use a.empty, a.bool(), a.item(), a.any() or a.all().

This seems to be an issue involving how the `color_fill` attribute is interpreted.

Here is the minimal working example of the code:

    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import folium
    import geopandas as gpd
    import os

    german_cars_price=pd.read_csv('data/german_cars_price.csv',index_col=0)
    federal_state_borders = gpd.read_file(os.path.join('data', 'federal_state_borders.geojson'))

    m = folium.Map(location=[52.5194, 13.4067], tiles='Mapbox Bright', zoom_start=5)
    m.choropleth(geo_data=federal_state_borders, key_on=federal_state_borders.VARNAME_1, data=german_cars_price, columns=['state', 'price'], fill_color='YlGn')

It seems fairly likely of course that I am doing something incorrectly, but it is unclear to me based on the documentation.

**Caveat lector:** this is probably my mistake as an end-user rather than a bug of the software.

I have not found the problem documented anywhere, and [the example in the documentation](http://python-visualization.github.io/folium/quickstart.html#Choropleth-maps) is too sparse (it does not specify the required formats of the data, or show what the structure of the arguments being passed to choropleth() in that call are) to deduce a solution. In particular, what `feature` is supposed to refer to, in either the example or in `folium.py`, is beyond me.

issue_title: Support field names that aren't valid Python identifiers
issue_body:
I want to support a field called "from", but I can't do this in marshmallow because it would cause a syntax error:

```python
class MySchema(Schema):
	from = fields.Nested(OtherSchema, attribute="my_attribute")
```

This got me thinking -- marshmallow is kind of backwards, isn't it?  The field that comes in from the outside world is the one that has to be specified as a valid Python identifier, and the field that you're actually going to work with in your code is specified as a string.  Shouldn't they be swapped?  I'm sure other folks are going to want to parse in or write out a value that isn't a valid Python identifier sometimes.

```python
class MySchema(Schema):
	my_attribute = fields.Nested(OtherSchema, field="from")
```

This could even be made backward compatible with existing Marshmallow stuff, like so:

- If neither "field" nor "attribute" are specified, the field and attribute values are both the same as the lvalue, so there's no issue.
- If "attribute" is specified, the lvalue is used for "field"
- if "field" is specified, the lvalue is used for "attribute"

Probably harder for you to actually implement this sorta thing though, but it's a thought.

issue_title: Prepare the Blockstack REST API for quick setup
issue_body:
@jcnelson and I just went through setting up the Blockstack REST API.

I'm using this issue to document the issues that we came across that need to be addressed for a programmatic setup:

- [ ] `blockstack rpcctl start` should be available without advanced mode turned on
- [ ] `blockstack rpcctl start` should not prompt the user to enter an email address
- [ ] `blockstack rpcctl start` should work without requiring a change to the `client.ini` file ("authenticate_api = false" could be set as the default)

---

Afterwards, @muneeb-ali and @jcnelson and I discussed a way that we could make the setup commands simple while still preserving the independence of Blockstack core from clients that communicate with it.

Here's an interface we came up with that should be work well:

```
blockstack setup
blockstack start api --key=be22dbf4305f45e082ce2e36d090968c2aacd712
```

The first command sets up the wallet while the second command starts the REST API.

Would love @larrysalibra's thoughts on this as well.

issue_title: Live searchbox shows with scroll bars on Users/Groups panel
issue_labels: [{'id': 65340100, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/plone/plonetheme.barceloneta/labels/01%20type:%20bug', 'name': '01 type: bug', 'color': 'fa0000', 'default': False}, {'id': 274278952, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/plone/plonetheme.barceloneta/labels/13%20prio:%20normal', 'name': '13 prio: normal', 'color': 'e06666', 'default': False}, {'id': 213994863, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/plone/plonetheme.barceloneta/labels/31%20needs:%20help', 'name': '31 needs: help', 'color': '00fafa', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
On the Users and Groups control panel, both the Users tab and the Groups tab, the live searchbox needlessly shows with scroll bars:

![screen shot 2018-05-22 at 12 24 05](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/210587/40357030-38919dfe-5dbb-11e8-888b-3112105cab92.png)

This is caused by commit d006020a5e77fb7151ea4f754f4932546aa0ea52, which has been there since Plone 5.0.0, and is still there in 5.1.2 (or current coredev 5.0 and 5.1).
Best solution is probably to make the css identifier more specific: only set the overflow on forms within the id `content-core`.

Additionally, this may be useful, to make the remaining users/groups form prettier:
- Set `overflow` to `auto`, because currently the scroll bars show even when no scroll bar is needed.
- Only set `overflow-x`, because I don't think we mind that the list is long vertically. We only care about the horizontal overflow, because it may disappear behind a portlet (https://github.com/plone/Products.CMFPlone/issues/879).

I'd like to introduce an OpenFisca command-line client.

Use cases:

```
$ openfisca test:yaml embauche.sgmap.fr/test_embauche_55c1dbb46149d1903b3d1e27.yaml
$ openfisca json-test-case-to-vectors <file_path>

The current parameter syntax is very heavy, due to its format being XML. Its vocabulary is poorly readable and made mostly of French words and abbreviations. Some attributes are inconsistent. Example: ```xml # Suppression du bouclier fiscal à compter des revenus réalisés en 2011. # ``` This proposal aims at making it much easier to contribute to the parameters, by: 1. Improving their readability. 2. Decreasing the amount of purely syntactic tokens. 3. Unifying the vocabulary with the rest of OpenFisca (engine & API). 4. Improving diff readability. ## 1 - references There is currently no mechanism for storing value-level references. This is obviously making some critical information miss, and leads to dirty workarounds (see https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/issues/766) and lost contributors (see https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/pull/728#issuecomment-304023834). ### Proposition A ```yaml bouclier_fiscal.taux: description: Bouclier fiscal type: percentage values: 2006-01-01: 0.6 2007-01-01: 0.5 2011-01-01: null references: 2006-01-01: http://example.org 2007-01-01: 2011-01-01: http://example.org ``` ### Proposition B ```yaml bouclier_fiscal.taux: description: Bouclier fiscal type: percentage values: 2006-01-01: value: 0.6 reference: http://example.org 2007-01-01: value: 0.6 2011-01-01: value: 0.5 reference: http://example.org ``` ## 2 - names _(assuming format 1B for references)_ ### Proposition A Inline hierarchy in the ID: ```yaml bouclier_fiscal.taux: description: Bouclier fiscal type: percentage values: 2006-01-01: value: 0.6 reference: http://example.org 2007-01-01: value: 0.6 2011-01-01: value: 0.5 reference: http://example.org ``` ### Proposition B Keep the current hierarchy: ```yaml bouclier_fiscal: taux: description: Bouclier fiscal type: percentage values: 2006-01-01: value: 0.6 reference: http://example.org 2007-01-01: value: 0.6 2011-01-01: value: 0.5 reference: http://example.org ``` Means there would be a few reserved names in order to identify leaves (such as `description`, `type`, `values`). ### Proposition C Rely on filesystem hierarchy: ```yaml # bouclier_fiscal.yaml taux: description: Bouclier fiscal type: percentage values: 2006-01-01: value: 0.6 reference: http://example.org 2007-01-01: value: 0.6 2011-01-01: value: 0.5 reference: http://example.org ``` ## Open questions 1. What are the [`conflicts` attributes](https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/pull/749#discussion_r118240100) used for? Can they be dropped? If not, how to represent them? 2. Can the `origin` attributes be absorbed in the `reference` attributes? I.e. can an opaque identifier be systematically superseded by an URI? (I frame it this way to show that it is definitely technically feasible, and that the question is purely semantic) 3. What is the exact correspondence table between the current `type` + `format` attributes couple and the new `type` attribute? Is it bijective? 4. Can this new format be thought of without taking into account table parameters (https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-core/issues/473)? If not, how can we handle them?

issue_title: Track Web API version in Piwik stats
issue_body: Relates to openfisca/openfisca-core#685 Requires openfisca/tracker/issues/4 **For** maintainers. **Story:** When deploying new API versions, I want to know the usage metrics for each of the currently supported versions, so I can provide a better overall experience to our users. **Motivations:** - Know which version we're going to keep running - Know when to notify of sunsetting to our users - Optimise our server usage, i.e. to avoid memory issues that could block all of our services **Definition of done:** - [x] Track API version for each tracked API request, for the versions that support it. - [x] Group API calls by version **Details:** In order to check web API versions usage, we can track the API version in the request url sent to Piwik. To do so, see `request.url` in the core web api module (`app.py`).

issue_title: Container names should have at least 2 characters
issue_body:
Trying to create a new container and letting NailGun generate its name, I got the following:

```
nailgun.client: DEBUG: Making HTTP POST request to https://satserver.com/docker/api/v2/containers with options {'verify': False, 'auth': ('admin', 'changeme'), 'headers': {'content-type': 'application/json'}} and data {"container": {"repository_name": "busybox", "compute_resource_id": 24, "tag": "latest", "command": "top", "organization_ids": [77], "name": "V"}}.
nailgun.client: WARNING: Received HTTP 422 response: {"errors":[["Error creating communicating with Docker. Check the Foreman logs: Invalid container name (V), only [a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9_.-] are allowed\n"]],"full_messages":[["Error creating communicating with Docker. Check the Foreman logs: Invalid container name (V), only [a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9_.-] are allowed\n"]]}
```

As the name is a random value, the above issue is not common to happen. The Docker error message states the name must follow the format `[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9_.-]`, in other words, it should have at least two characters. With that said the minimal length of a container name should be 2 and not 1 as the StringField uses as default.

issue_title: out of memory while retrieving all objects in a table
issue_body:
I have about 700 thousand objects in a table and i want to retrieve all of them.
So, i do this:
```python
all_objects = Person.objects.limit(1000000).all()
for item in all_objects:
    #do something
```
Here come the problems.
1.  it took a little time to execute the first line and it's not a big problem. 
2.  the little program ate about 4g of my memory and finally killed by the system.

I went through the source code and i found two related methods in class AbstractQuerySet :
*  
```python
    def _execute_query(self):
        if self._batch:
            raise CQLEngineException("Only inserts, updates, and deletes are available in batch mode")
        if self._result_cache is None:
            self._result_cache = list(self._execute(self._select_query()))
            self._construct_result = self._get_result_constructor()
```
so, you cached all the 700 thousand objects? 

*  
```python
    def __iter__(self):
        self._execute_query()

        for idx in range(len(self._result_cache)):
            instance = self._result_cache[idx]
            if isinstance(instance, dict):
                self._fill_result_cache_to_idx(idx)
            yield self._result_cache[idx]
```
1. i think it's better to use range in six.moves ...
2. the usage of memory kept increasing while iterating, i think it may have something to do with the `_fill_result_cache_to_idx`

issue_title: No way to slow down the scrapy speed?
issue_body:
In README.rst, it only says the `DOWNLOAD_DELAY` will behave in unexpected way, but no alternative suggestion.

So how to control the crawling speed while using splash?

Hello,

I'm using the following configuration to:

 1. Create a snapshot
 2. Remove older indices

```yml
---
actions:
  1:
    action: snapshot
    description: >-
      Create a snapshot with the last week index.
    options:
      repository: logstash
      # Leaving name blank will result in the default 'curator-%Y%m%d%H%M%S'
      name:
      ignore_unavailable: False
      include_global_state: True
      partial: False
      wait_for_completion: True
      skip_repo_fs_check: False
      continue_if_exception: False
      disable_action: False
    filters:
    - filtertype: pattern
      kind: prefix
      value: logstash-
      exclude:
    - filtertype: age
      source: creation_date
      direction: younger
      unit: weeks
      unit_count: 2
      exclude:
    - filtertype: age
      source: creation_date
      direction: younger
      unit: weeks
      unit_count: 1
      exclude: True
  2:
    action: delete_indices
    description: >-
      Remove indices starting with 'logstash-' older than 5 weeks
    options:
      ignore_empty_list: True
      continue_if_exception: False
      disable_action: False
    filters:
    - filtertype: pattern
      kind: prefix
      value: logstash-
      exclude:

Curator 4.0.6 will start and complete with invalid top level directives.  Additionally, no error logs are provided as evidence of invalid configuration.

This can provide a poor experience for new & experienced users.  E.g., the following will match & delete all indices, as `filter` (as opposed to the correct `filters`) is ignored.

```
actions:
  1
    action: delete_indices
    description: >-
      Blow stuff away
    options:
      continue_if_exception: False
      disable_action: False
    filter:
    - filtertype: pattern
      kind: prefix
      value: filebeat-
    - filtertype: age
      source: name
      timestring: '%Y-%m-%d'
      direction: older
      unit: days
      unit_count: 2
      exclude:
```

Hi there, this may just be a case of something caused by our internal setup, but I have a pmml of an xgboost model generated by sklearn2pmml.  The response variable is float and binary, and i with what I assume is a somewhat recent change, the regression tables in the regression model section name the targetCategory with `.0` in the literals:

```
					<RegressionTable intercept="0.0" targetCategory="1.0">
						<NumericPredictor name="xgbValue" coefficient="1.0"/>
					</RegressionTable>
					<RegressionTable intercept="0.0" targetCategory="0.0"/>
				</RegressionModel>
```
Our evaluation system uses jpmml-evaluator 1.3.1 and with a pmml specified like the above, the evaluated output is all zeros. If the literals don't have decimal places, it is happy and produces nonzero outputs with the expected values:
```
					<RegressionTable intercept="0.0" targetCategory="1">
						<NumericPredictor name="xgbValue" coefficient="1.0"/>
					</RegressionTable>
					<RegressionTable intercept="0.0" targetCategory="0"/>
				</RegressionModel>
```
I realize we are using an older pmml-evaluator version but the evaluation team is cagey about upgrading.  Is there an easy way to address this, like by displaying literals without decimal places when possible?  Casting the response to integer before training resolves the issue also but we would need to start doing that across a lot of models. Thanks for any insight you can offer.

Hello,

I'm a junior developer and I'm playing around with the Mapbox CLI. I installed through Homebrew with no problems, but when I tried setting the token as per the instructions here on Github:

`$ mapbox --access-token tokenfromstudiohomepagegoeshere12323342423442`

I'm getting 

    Usage: mapbox [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...
    Error: Missing command.

Is there something basic I'm missing here?

The `_geojson` method of the `Directions` class creates a GeoJSON FeatureCollection from the data in the body of a response object.

If the geometry of the FeatureCollection is "LineString", then `_geojson` calls `polyline.decode`.

`polyline.decode` returns coordinates as latitude, longitude tuples, where the GeoJSON standard requires longitude, latitude arrays.

This can make it difficult for a user to create a static map with the FeatureCollection returned by `_geojson`.  (It can, for example, result in validation errors.)

Unless it would have unintended consequences, I propose to make a small change to `_geojson`.

__Current:__

```
'coordinates': polyline.decode(route['geometry'])
```

__Proposed (where "pair" is shorthand for "coordinate pair"):__
```
'coordinates': [
  list(reversed(pair)) for pair in polyline.decode(route['geometry'])
]
```

Incidentally, this also has implications for the `Optimization` class (mapbox/mapbox-sdk-py/pull/241).

issue_title: types: possible to support both JSON/JSONB
issue_body:
Currently SQLAlchemy has JSONB support for PostgreSQL:
http://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/latest/dialects/postgresql.html#sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql.JSONB

However, it doesn't seem like it will go into JSONType in SQLAlchemy-Utils due to difficulties in making it behave nicely across DBs:
https://github.com/kvesteri/sqlalchemy-utils/pull/125

For Invenio-Records we could likely get along with using JSONB when on PostgreSQL (and stay with text no the other platforms), as all we basically do is get and put the entire JSON so comparators is less of an issue.

issue_title: api: reindex doesn't work if the queue is not initialized
issue_body:
**Problem**:
If, after install invenio3, I run:
```
invenio3 index reindex --yes-i-know
invenio3 index run
```
It doesn't index the records because the queue is not previously declared.
What we need to do is [declare the queue](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-indexer/blob/master/tests/conftest.py#L96-L101) the first time we initialize the system.

**Solution proposed**:
We can add a signal inside the `invenio-search` cli `init` and `destroy`; in the same time, in `invenio-indexer` register a signal to `initialize` and `destroy` the queue every time the indices are created or destroyed.

----------------------------------------------------------------------
cc @lnielsen @jirikuncar

issue_body:
The following cookiecutter change:

   https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98

should be propagated to this Invenio module.

Namely, in `run-tests.sh`, the sphinx for doctests is invoked after pytest run:

```console
$ tail -3 ./\{\{\ cookiecutter.project_shortname\ \}\}/run-tests.sh
sphinx-build -qnNW docs docs/_build/html && python setup.py test && sphinx-build -qnNW -b doctest docs docs/_build/doctest
```

This sometimes led to problems on Travis CI with the second sphinx-build run due
to "disappearing" dependencies after the example application was tested.

A solution that worked for invenio-marc21 (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49#issuecomment-281287103)
and that was integrated in cookiecutter (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98) was to
run doctest execution in pytest, removing the second `sphinx-build` invocation.

This both solved Travis CI build failures and simplified test suite execution.

Note that this change may necessitate to amend the code tests etc so that things
would be executed with the Flask application context (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49/commits/09e98fc18fd5591dc65d806286e8da51b7e384b4).

- introduce new documentation structure https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/issues/56
- document user perspective
- document signals (if not done)
- document example app (if not done)

issue_title: Issues with running under another virtualenv + fish shell and a workaround
issue_body:
I am using fish shell + project virtualenv.

Whenever I activate my project's virtualenv, running pre-commit would fail, with strange errors like `basename: command not found'.

`sh
/Users/ustun/.pre-commit/repoUMoyOJ/py_env-default/bin/activate: line 61: basename: command not found
bash: xargs: command not found
`

However, after deactivating the virtualenv, pre-commit would run properly.

It turns out one of the reasons is the usage of `bash -c`.

fish sets path as space delimited values: /usr/bin /usr/local/bin as opposed to /usr/bin:/usr/local/bin.

When we run bash -c 'echo $PATH' for example, it still receives the $PATH of fish, not $bash.

I went ahead and replaced in my local pre-commit scripts everything that refers to bash with fish, and changes &&'s to ;

This fixed it for me. Just documenting in case it helps another person.

I'm still not sure why deactivating the project virtualenv helped, I guess there's some incompatibility by running activate.fish (fish specific script for venv) and then activate (bash specific script for venv).

I also tried bash -l -c to get a clean bash environment, but that didn't help.

issue_title: Obtuse exception when a batch has only an empty sentence
issue_body:
Data (`sentences.txt`):

```
Call me Ishmael.
Some years ago- never mind how long precisely- having little or no money in my purse, and nothing particular to interest me on shore, I thought I would sail about a little and see the watery part of the world.
It is a way I have of driving off the spleen and regulating the circulation.
Whenever I find myself growing grim about the mouth; whenever it is a damp, drizzly November in my soul; whenever I find myself involuntarily pausing before coffin warehouses, and bringing up the rear of every funeral I meet; and especially whenever my hypos get such an upper hand of me, that it requires a strong moral principle to prevent me from deliberately stepping into the street, and methodically knocking people's hats off- then, I account it high time to get to sea as soon as I can.
This is my substitute for pistol and ball.
With a philosophical flourish Cato throws himself upon his sword; I quietly take to the ship.
There is nothing surprising in this.
If they but knew it, almost all men in their degree, some time or other, cherish very nearly the same feelings towards the ocean with me.
There now is your insular city of the Manhattoes, belted round by wharves as Indian isles by coral reefs- commerce surrounds it with her surf.
Right and left, the streets take you waterward.
Its extreme downtown is the battery, where that noble mole is washed by waves, and cooled by breezes, which a few hours previous were out of sight of land.
Look at the crowds of water-gazers there.
Circumambulate the city of a dreamy Sabbath afternoon.
Go from Corlears Hook to Coenties Slip, and from thence, by Whitehall, northward.
What do you see?- Posted like silent sentinels all around the town, stand thousands upon thousands of mortal men fixed in ocean reveries.
Some leaning against the spiles; some seated upon the pier-heads; some looking over the bulwarks of ships from China; some high aloft in the rigging, as if striving to get a still better seaward peep.
But these are all landsmen; of week days pent up in lath and plaster- tied to counters, nailed to benches, clinched to desks.
How then is this? Are the green fields gone? What do they here?
But look! here come more crowds, pacing straight for the water, and seemingly bound for a dive.
Strange! Nothing will content them but the extremest limit of the land; loitering under the shady lee of yonder warehouses will not suffice.
No.
They must get just as nigh the water as they possibly can without falling And there they stand- miles of them- leagues.
Inlanders all, they come from lanes and alleys, streets avenues- north, east, south, and west.
Yet here they all unite.
Tell me, does the magnetic virtue of the needles of the compasses of all those ships attract them thither?
Once more.
Say you are in the country; in some high land of lakes.
Take almost any path you please, and ten to one it carries you down in a dale, and leaves you there by a pool in the stream.
There is magic in it.
Let the most absent-minded of men be plunged in his deepest reveries- stand that man on his legs, set his feet a-going, and he will infallibly lead you to water, if water there be in all that region.
Should you ever be athirst in the great American desert, try this experiment, if your caravan happen to be supplied with a metaphysical professor.
Yes, as every one knows, meditation and water are wedded for ever.
But here is an artist.
He desires to paint you the dreamiest, shadiest, quietest, most enchanting bit of romantic landscape in all the valley of the Saco.
What is the chief element he employs? There stand his trees, each with a hollow trunk, as if a hermit and a crucifix were within; and here sleeps his meadow, and there sleep his cattle; and up from yonder cottage goes a sleepy smoke.
Deep into distant woodlands winds a mazy way, reaching to overlapping spurs of mountains bathed in their hill-side blue.
But though the picture lies thus tranced, and though this pine-tree shakes down its sighs like leaves upon this shepherd's head, yet all were vain, unless the shepherd's eye were fixed upon the magic stream before him.
Go visit the Prairies in June, when for scores on scores of miles you wade knee-deep among Tiger-lilies- what is the one charm wanting?- Water- there is not a drop of water there! Were Niagara but a cataract of sand, would you travel your thousand miles to see it? Why did the poor poet of Tennessee, upon suddenly receiving two handfuls of silver, deliberate whether to buy him a coat, which he sadly needed, or invest his money in a pedestrian trip to Rockaway Beach? Why is almost every robust healthy boy with a robust healthy soul in him, at some time or other crazy to go to sea? Why upon your first voyage as a passenger, did you yourself feel such a mystical vibration, when first told that you and your ship were now out of sight of land? Why did the old Persians hold the sea holy? Why did the Greeks give it a separate deity, and own brother of Jove? Surely all this is not without meaning.
And still deeper the meaning of that story of Narcissus, who because he could not grasp the tormenting, mild image he saw in the fountain, plunged into it and was drowned.
But that same image, we ourselves see in all rivers and oceans.
It is the image of the ungraspable phantom of life; and this is the key to it all.
Now, when I say that I am in the habit of going to sea whenever I begin to grow hazy about the eyes, and begin to be over conscious of my lungs, I do not mean to have it inferred that I ever go to sea as a passenger.
For to go as a passenger you must needs have a purse, and a purse is but a rag unless you have something in it.
Besides, passengers get sea-sick- grow quarrelsome- don't sleep of nights- do not enjoy themselves much, as a general thing;- no, I never go as a passenger; nor, though I am something of a salt, do I ever go to sea as a Commodore, or a Captain, or a Cook.
I abandon the glory and distinction of such offices to those who like them.
For my part, I abominate all honorable respectable toils, trials, and tribulations of every kind whatsoever.
It is quite as much as I can do to take care of myself, without taking care of ships, barques, brigs, schooners, and what not.
And as for going as cook,- though I confess there is considerable glory in that, a cook being a sort of officer on ship-board- yet, somehow, I never fancied broiling fowls;- though once broiled, judiciously buttered, and judgmatically salted and peppered, there is no one who will speak more respectfully, not to say reverentially, of a broiled fowl than I will.
It is out of the idolatrous dotings of the old Egyptians upon broiled ibis and roasted river horse, that you see the mummies of those creatures in their huge bakehouses the pyramids.
No, when I go to sea, I go as a simple sailor, right before the mast, plumb down into the fore-castle, aloft there to the royal mast-head.
True, they rather order me about some, and make me jump from spar to spar, like a grasshopper in a May meadow.
And at first, this sort of thing is unpleasant enough.
It touches one's sense of honor, particularly if you come of an old established family in the land, the Van Rensselaers, or Randolphs, or Hardicanutes.
And more than all, if just previous to putting your hand into the tar-pot, you have been lording it as a country schoolmaster, making the tallest boys stand in awe of you.
The transition is a keen one, I assure you, from a schoolmaster to a sailor, and requires a strong decoction of Seneca and the Stoics to enable you to grin and bear it.
But even this wears off in time.
What of it, if some old hunks of a sea-captain orders me to get a broom and sweep down the decks? What does that indignity amount to, weighed, I mean, in the scales of the New Testament? Do you think the archangel Gabriel thinks anything the less of me, because I promptly and respectfully obey that old hunks in that particular instance? Who ain't a slave? Tell me that.
Well, then, however the old sea-captains may order me about- however they may thump and punch me about, I have the satisfaction of knowing that it is all right; that everybody else is one way or other served in much the same way- either in a physical or metaphysical point of view, that is; and so the universal thump is passed round, and all hands should rub each other's shoulder-blades, and be content.
Again, I always go to sea as a sailor, because they make a point of paying me for my trouble, whereas they never pay passengers a single penny that I ever heard of.
On the contrary, passengers themselves must pay.
And there is all the difference in the world between paying and being paid.
The act of paying is perhaps the most uncomfortable infliction that the two orchard thieves entailed upon us.
But being paid,- what will compare with it? The urbane activity with which a man receives money is really marvellous, considering that we so earnestly believe money to be the root of all earthly ills, and that on no account can a monied man enter heaven.
Ah! how cheerfully we consign ourselves to perdition!
Finally, I always go to sea as a sailor, because of the wholesome exercise and pure air of the fore-castle deck.
For as in this world, head winds are far more prevalent than winds from astern (that is, if you never violate the Pythagorean maxim), so for the most part the Commodore on the quarter-deck gets his atmosphere at second hand from the sailors on the forecastle.
He thinks he breathes it first; but not so.
In much the same way do the commonalty lead their leaders in many other things, at the same time that the leaders little suspect it.
But wherefore it was that after having repeatedly smelt the sea as a merchant sailor, I should now take it into my head to go on a whaling voyage; this the invisible police officer of the Fates, who has the constant surveillance of me, and secretly dogs me, and influences me in some unaccountable way- he can better answer than any one else.
And, doubtless, my going on this whaling voyage, formed part of the grand programme of Providence that was drawn up a long time ago.
It came in as a sort of brief interlude and solo between more extensive performances.
I take it that this part of the bill must have run something like this:
"Grand Contested Election for the Presidency of the United States.
"WHALING VOYAGE BY ONE ISHMAEL.
" "BLOODY BATTLE IN AFFGHANISTAN.
"
Though I cannot tell why it was exactly that those stage managers, the Fates, put me down for this shabby part of a whaling voyage, when others were set down for magnificent parts in high tragedies, and short and easy parts in genteel comedies, and jolly parts in farces- though I cannot tell why this was exactly; yet, now that I recall all the circumstances, I think I can see a little into the springs and motives which being cunningly presented to me under various disguises, induced me to set about performing the part I did, besides cajoling me into the delusion that it was a choice resulting from my own unbiased freewill and discriminating judgment.
Chief among these motives was the overwhelming idea of the great whale himself.
Such a portentous and mysterious monster roused all my curiosity.
Then the wild and distant seas where he rolled his island bulk; the undeliverable, nameless perils of the whale; these, with all the attending marvels of a thousand Patagonian sights and sounds, helped to sway me to my wish.
With other men, perhaps, such things would not have been inducements; but as for me, I am tormented with an everlasting itch for things remote.
I love to sail forbidden seas, and land on barbarous coasts.
Not ignoring what is good, I am quick to perceive a horror, and could still be social with it- would they let me- since it is but well to be on friendly terms with all the inmates of the place one lodges in.
By reason of these things, then, the whaling voyage was welcome; the great flood-gates of the wonder-world swung open, and in the wild conceits that swayed me to my purpose, two and two there floated into my inmost soul, endless processions of the whale, and, mid most of them all, one grand hooded phantom, like a snow hill in the air.

```
